text.content: 2.1  Scale Comparison 1 5 Vs  [s2_1_scale_comparison_1_5_vs]

rich_text.plain_text: 2.1  Scale Comparison 1 5 Vs  [s2_1_scale_comparison_1_5_vs]

text.content: Left vertical picture graph appears. Student's data shown with scale of 5. Key: each star symbol = 5.

rich_text.plain_text: Left vertical picture graph appears. Student's data shown with scale of 5. Key: each star symbol = 5.

text.content: Right vertical picture graph appears. Same data shown with scale of 10. Key: each star symbol = 10.

rich_text.plain_text: Right vertical picture graph appears. Same data shown with scale of 10. Key: each star symbol = 10.

text.content: "Here's your data two ways. Look at both graphs. Which graph uses fewer symbols to show the same data?"

rich_text.plain_text: "Here's your data two ways. Look at both graphs. Which graph uses fewer symbols to show the same data?"

text.content:
tool: multiple_choice  options: ["Left graph (scale of 5)", "Right graph (scale of 10)"]
"Which graph uses FEWER symbols to show the same data?"

rich_text.plain_text:
tool: multiple_choice  options: ["Left graph (scale of 5)", "Right graph (scale of 10)"]
"Which graph uses FEWER symbols to show the same data?"

text.content: ✅ Student selected the 1:10 graph, correct

rich_text.plain_text: ✅ Student selected the 1:10 graph, correct

text.content: Right graph highlights to confirm selection.

rich_text.plain_text: Right graph highlights to confirm selection.

text.content: "Right. Scale of 10 uses fewer symbols. Each symbol shows more, so you need fewer of them."

rich_text.plain_text: "Right. Scale of 10 uses fewer symbols. Each symbol shows more, so you need fewer of them."

text.content: ❌ Student selected the 1:5 graph, which uses more symbols

rich_text.plain_text: ❌ Student selected the 1:5 graph, which uses more symbols

text.content: Left graph highlights.

rich_text.plain_text: Left graph highlights.

text.content: Right graph highlights.

rich_text.plain_text: Right graph highlights.

text.content: "Look at the keys. When each symbol stands for more, you need fewer symbols. Which key shows a bigger number?"

rich_text.plain_text: "Look at the keys. When each symbol stands for more, you need fewer symbols. Which key shows a bigger number?"

text.content: ❌ Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

rich_text.plain_text: ❌ Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

text.content: Right graph highlights to show the answer.

rich_text.plain_text: Right graph highlights to show the answer.

text.content: "Let me show you. The right graph has scale of 10. Each symbol stands for 10. The left graph has scale of 5. Each symbol stands for 5. When each symbol shows more, you need fewer symbols to show the same amount. The right graph uses fewer symbols because its scale is bigger."

rich_text.plain_text: "Let me show you. The right graph has scale of 10. Each symbol stands for 10. The left graph has scale of 5. Each symbol stands for 5. When each symbol shows more, you need fewer symbols to show the same amount. The right graph uses fewer symbols because its scale is bigger."

text.content: ⏭️ Student presses Next

rich_text.plain_text: ⏭️ Student presses Next

text.content: "With scale of 10, each symbol shows more than scale of 5. That's why you need fewer symbols for the same data."

rich_text.plain_text: "With scale of 10, each symbol shows more than scale of 5. That's why you need fewer symbols for the same data."
